body: I just copied your configuration and tried it on my system (_macOS_) and it works as it should: ```bash starship 1.16.0 branch: commit_hash: build_time:2023-07-30 13:30:05 +00:00 build_env:rustc 1.71.0 (8ede3aae2 2023-07-12) (built from a source tarball), ```

@mickimnet Sounds good! I tried this setup on Arch-based systems and it worked there too. But I'm not sure why the flag isn't working on my current setup. I looked at the source code to find where the flag's value might be reset to its default, but I didn't find anything. Any thoughts on how to investigate this? Maybe trying debug flags or more detailed logs could help?

I believe I've located where the problem might be coming from:

<details>

<summary>env STARSHIP_LOG=trace starship module battery output</summary>


I found some related [sources](https://github.com/starship/starship/blob/173486f85cfa0a31ddb2aa10d444ae5ef5845c8f/src/modules/battery.rs#L70). It's interesting to know what's happening under the hood, especially because `upower` is giving me the right information...
Command:
```shell
$ upower -i $(upower -e | grep 'BAT') | grep -E "state|to\ full|percentage"
```
Output:
```shell
    state:               discharging
    percentage:          92%
```
yeah 2 years and still no solution, i have the same problem too(arch linux)
